title: Problem 1
statement_text: Let $p\geq 1$ be a fixed rational number. Instance: A (multi-)digraph $D$. Decide whether $\vec{\chi}^{\ast}(D)\leq p$.
context_text: The problem of determining the complexity of deciding whether the star dichromatic number of a digraph is at most $p$ was posed by Hochstättler and Steiner (2019). For $p=1$ the problem reduces to deciding acyclicity, solvable in linear time; the hardness for $p>1$ was left open.
paper_contribution: Proves NP-completeness for every fixed rational $p>1$, fully resolving the open problem.
paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity

title: Problem 2.15
statement_text: Let $F$ be a fixed (multi-)digraph. Instance: A (multi-)digraph $D$. Decide whether $D$ is circularly $F$-colourable.
context_text: A digraph $D$ is circularly $F$-colourable if there exists a circular homomorphism from $D$ to $F$. This problem generalises Problem 1 and serves as a directed analogue of the $H$-colouring problem for graphs. Conjecture 2.16 asserts that NP-completeness holds whenever $F$ contains a directed cycle, and Theorem 2.17 establishes this for almost all such cases.
notes: The complete dichotomy (all F with a directed cycle) is conjectured but not fully proved within the paper.
paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity

title: Conjecture 2.16
statement_text: Let $F$ be a digraph which contains a directed cycle. Then the circular $F$-colouring problem is NP-complete.
context_text: The authors conjecture that, under the assumption $\mathsf{P}\neq\mathsf{NP}$, the only polynomially solvable instances of Problem 2.15 are those where $F$ is acyclic (for which only acyclic digraphs map circularly to $F$). Theorem 2.17 confirms this conjecture for almost all cases.
paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity

title: Problem 3.21
statement_text: Let $p\geq 1$ be a fixed real number. Instance: A (multi-)digraph $D$. Decide whether $\vec{\chi}_{f}(D)\leq p$.
context_text: This problem asks for the complexity of deciding whether the fractional dichromatic number of a digraph is at most $p$. The paper proves NP-completeness for all real $p>1$ with $p\neq 2$, mirroring results of Feder et al. for the circular dichromatic number; the case $p=2$ is explicitly left open.
notes: The case $p=2$ remains unresolved after this paper.
paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity

title: Conjecture 5.38 (Jaeger)
statement_text: Any planar graph $G$ of girth at least $4k$ admits a homomorphism to $C_{2k+1}$. In other words, $\chi_{c}(G)\leq\frac{2k+1}{k}$.
context_text: This well-known conjecture on homomorphism bounds for planar graphs of large girth is attributed to Jaeger (1984). It contains as a special case for $k=1$ Grötzsch's theorem that every triangle-free planar graph is 3-colourable. The paper cites it as motivation for the directed analogue Conjecture 5.39.
paper_contribution: Mentions the conjecture as direct motivation for posing a directed analogue (Conjecture 5.39) in the framework of circular homomorphisms between digraphs; no new progress on the conjecture itself is claimed.
paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity

title: Conjecture 5.39
statement_text: Any planar digraph $D$ of directed girth at least $k\geq 3$ admits a circular homomorphism to $\vec{C}_{k-1}$. In other words, $\vec{\chi}^{\ast}(D)\leq\frac{k-1}{k-2}$.
context_text: Posed by Hochstättler and Steiner (2019) as a directed analogue of Jaeger's conjecture for graphs. The case $k=3$ corresponds to the 2-Colour-Conjecture of Neumann-Lara, and $k=4$ gives a directed analogue of Grötzsch's theorem for oriented planar $\vec{C}_{3}$-free digraphs.
paper_contribution: Introduces the notion of circular homomorphisms between digraphs as the natural framework in which this conjecture is formulated, and develops the theory of circular $F$-colourability (Section 2) that underpins the conjecture.
paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity

title: Problem 5.40
statement_text: For given $k,b\in\mathbb{N}$ with $k\geq b$, is there a directed graph $\vec{K}(k,b)$ with vertex set $\binom{[k]}{b}$ such that the following holds? The subdigraph of $\vec{K}(k,b)$ induced by any $\{B_{1},\ldots,B_{l}\}\subseteq\binom{[k]}{b}$ is acylic if and only if $\bigcap_{i=1}^{l}{B_{i}}\neq\emptyset$.
context_text: Such 'directed Kneser graphs' $\vec{K}(k,b)$ would allow $b$-tuple $k$-colourings of digraphs to be equivalent to the existence of circular homomorphisms to $\vec{K}(k,b)$, tying the fractional dichromatic number to circular homomorphism theory. Existence is confirmed for $k=b+1$ via the directed cycle of length $b+1$, but the general case is open.
paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity
